text: When working on a project with Git, branching is a powerful way to manage changes. Instead of editing the main code directly, developers create feature branches to work on new functions or fixes. This keeps the main branch stable. Once the feature is complete, the changes are combined back through merging. Before merging, teams often use pull requests to review the code and discuss improvements. This creates a clear version control workflow. Overall, branching helps teams collaborate without conflicts, making it easier to track progress and keep the project organized.
